item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely loved reading this. Maybe it's because I'm partial to anything Pentagram, but Alan was a fun character to follow in his own right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always imagined him as stark white like the wiki, so I guess Slenderman with a mouth? But I like the sound of eyeless Humphrey Bogart too. Honestly might be more believable to explain Hollywood big names overlooking his appearance. So I'm gonna go with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite like the concept of a sort of pataphysical narratively driven entity whose anomaly can be controlled by print media. It's a fairly interesting thing. But I have one question to help me visualize this thing. When you say "white" do you mean caucasian or white like the background of the wiki? I'm trying to decide whether it's eyeless Humphry Bogart and Slenderman cosplaying Humphry Bogart

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well that’s incorrect. It’s not an acronym.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dunno, it seems like PENTAGRAM is becoming more of the official term/name than just 'Pentagram'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think much of the dialogue was ever set up to make the protagonist (Alan) particularly compelling, which makes the ending non-impactful.

item.title: Fresh
item.content:encoded:
I love this SCP.

You recognized your strength and twisted the medium to suit it. The infohazard at play was fresh and the ending helped recontextualize the whole piece. I particularly love the us of The Pentagram as an antagonistic force, very refreshing to see a lesser used GOI.

I do agree that most of Alan's personality is only interesting because of the almost noir touch to it. I would like to see him have a little bit agency in his story. It feels as if he's just being pushed around by one organization to the next. Alan is visually interesting but it's hard to connect his difference to his ability, it seems a little random given the context.

All in all, it feels fresh but needs a little bit of polish. You get my upvote.

item.title: Re: Lookalike SCP
item.content:encoded: seconded, I believe that this SCP takes a unique approach to infohazards.

item.title: Re: Lookalike SCP
item.content:encoded: … How is this similar? 2521 doesn't have a monopoly on infohazardous properties, and this article doesn't have any other notable similarities besides "activating when written about".

item.title: Lookalike SCP
item.content:encoded: This SCP does remain ind me of SCP-2521 which is an anomaly that manifests if anyone mentions it. Otherwise it’s still an undecided vote from me. Still though, good writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Pentagram (not PENTAGRAM) is used well and Smithee shooting Sandy a la Indiana Jones is inspired. Unfortunately, Smithee just isn't an interesting character. His dialogue doesn't entertain and the ending doesn't do anything for me either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first SCP. I fancy myself a screenwriter because dialogue is my strong suit. I chose X323 because 323 is an area code for the Los Angeles area—213 is the area code for Hollywood, but I was beaten to that number, so I'll take what I can get.

I won't lie and say I'm using PENTAGRAM because of any reason other than trying to avoid using the Chaos Insurgency; but it all works out. I'm quite fond of the idea that the occult branch of the DoD used the Red Scare in 1950s Hollywood as an excuse to abduct and experiment on low level blacklisted figures. If I get anymore good ideas, this may be the first of a "Hollywood Horrors" or "Hollyweird" series.

Special thanks to:
TrustyOlValet2 – critted both old and new draft, gave opinions on adding or not adding particular elements
Its a Bad Idea – critted both drafts
Nerdibbles – critted old draft
not_a_seagull – critted old draft, inspired the rewrite
JackalRelated – gave screenwriting formatting advice, suggested the use of PENTAGRAM, created PENTAGRAM image
CadaverCommander – suggested the use of SCP-4333 (which they authored)
Cerastes – critted new draft, suggested the use of SCP-3928 (which they authored)
Dr Moned – critted new draft

Image credits:

  • Me, for the rating image (fashioned after the MPAA rating box but I typed in the text myself)
  • JackalRelated for the PENTAGRAM logo
